NBA Net Wins Analyzer

A career contribution metric by Will Fiore
226 players · all eras · ABA included · fully interactive
GitHub ↗
The Statistic
Net Wins — a player's contextual contribution to wins and losses
Traditional stats measure what a player does. Net Wins measures how much those actions are worth — normalized by what it actually costs a team to win or lose that season.
Player Wins
Player positive actions (PTS+REB+AST+BLK+STL) ÷ (Team positive actions ÷ Team wins)
Player Losses
Player negative actions (Missed FG+Missed FT+TOV+PF) ÷ (Team negative actions ÷ Team losses)
Net Wins = Player Wins − Player Losses
Example: Cade Cunningham, 2025-26 — 10.36 player wins, 3.44 player losses = 6.92 net wins
What this metric measures
Net Wins measures how efficiently a player's statistical contributions converted into winning and losing — normalized against what their specific team was doing that season. It is a contextual efficiency metric, not a causal wins-added stat.
What it doesn't claim
Net Wins does not isolate individual causation from team quality — a player on a 60-win team is measured against a 60-win context. Compare players across similar team environments, or use avg/season and peak metrics to adjust for this. This is a known limitation, not a bug.

Built by Will Fiore · github.com/willf123/nba-net-wins · © 2026
Built by Will Fiore · github.com/willf123/nba-net-wins
Net Wins is an original statistic. Player and team stats sourced from public NBA records. Pre-1974 BLK/STL estimated or editable. All calculations run client-side — no data is sent anywhere.
